title: To authorize the Bay Mills Indian Community of the State of Michigan to convey land and interests in land owned by the Tribe.
stage_label: Referred to the Subcommittee on Indian and Insular Affairs.
plain_summary: This bill gives the Bay Mills Indian Community in Michigan the right to sell or lease their own land without asking the federal government for permission first. The tribe can only do this with land they fully own, not with land that the US government holds in trust for them. If the tribe makes a bad deal or loses money from selling their land, the federal government will not have to pay them back or help fix the problem. This gives the tribe more control over their property while making sure the government is not responsible for any mistakes.

sections.title: Section 1  Land and Interests of the Bay Mills Indian Community of Michigan
sections.text: The Bay Mills Indian Community of Michigan can sell, lease, or give away any land they own that is not held in trust by the United States government. This includes any person or organization acting for the tribe. They do not need to get permission from the federal government first. However, this law does not allow the tribe to sell or lease any land that the United States holds in trust for them. Trust land is land that the federal government manages and protects for the benefit of the tribe. The United States government will not be responsible for any problems or losses that happen when the tribe sells or leases their land under this new law. The government will only be responsible if they are directly involved in the deal or if another law says they must be responsible. This protection does not apply if the tribe gives land to the United States to be held in trust for the tribe.
